age.Description: Age of the participant at MEG recording.

education_years.Description: Years of education of the participant.

BNT.Instructions: Subjects are asked to name objects that are presented visually as two-dimensional line drawings in a booklet. The score is the total number of objects that were identified within the first 20 seconds.
BNT.ReferencesAndLinks: Kaplan, E., Goodglass, H., & Weintraub, S. (2001). Boston Naming Test.

DST_forward.LongName: Digit Span Test (forward), Wechsler Memory Scale-III-R (Spanish version).
DST_forward.Instructions: Subjects are read sequences of numbers and are asked to repeat them in the same order. Sequence lengths go from 2 to 9 digits, with 2 trials per sequence length (thus a total of 16 sequences). The score is the number of correctly repeated sequences.
DST_forward.ReferencesAndLinks: Wechsler, D. (2004). WMS-III Escala de memoria de Wechsler-III. TEA ediciones.

DST_backward.LongName: Digit Span Test (backward), Wechsler Memory Scale-III-R (Spanish version).
DST_backward.Instructions: Subjects are read sequences of numbers and are asked to repeat them in reversed order. Sequence lengths go from 2 to 8 digits, with 2 trials per sequence length (thus a total of 14 sequences). The score is the number of correctly repeated sequences.
DST_backward.ReferencesAndLinks: Wechsler, D. (2004). WMS-III Escala de memoria de Wechsler-III. TEA ediciones.

imitation_postures.Instructions: Subjects are asked to imitate 8 hand gestures performed by the examiner. The score is the number of correct imitations.

LM_imm_units.LongName: Logical Memory (immediate units), Wechsler Memory Scale-III-R (Spanish version).
LM_imm_units.Instructions: Subjects listen to 2 short texts and are instructed to pay attention for later recall. The second text is repeated. For the score, a checklist of 25 specific ideas is used. Subjects get 1 point for each remembered idea. The total score is 25 points per text (here 75, as the second text is repeated).
LM_imm_units.ReferencesAndLinks: Wechsler, D. (2004). WMS-III Escala de memoria de Wechsler-III. TEA ediciones.

LM_del_units.LongName: Logical Memory (delayed units), Wechsler Memory Scale-III-R (Spanish version).
LM_del_units.Instructions: Subjects listen to 2 short texts and are instructed to pay attention for later recall. The second text is repeated. For the score, a checklist of 25 specific ideas is used 20 minutes after the reading. Subjects get 1 point for each remembered idea. The total score is 25 points per text (here 50).
LM_del_units.ReferencesAndLinks: Wechsler, D. (2004). WMS-III Escala de memoria de Wechsler-III. TEA ediciones.

LM_imm_them.LongName: Logical Memory (immediate thematic), Wechsler Memory Scale-III-R (Spanish version).
LM_imm_them.Instructions: Subjects listen to 2 short texts and are instructed to pay attention for later recall. The second text is repeated. For the score, a checklist of 7 general ideas is used. Subjects get 1 point for each remembered idea. The total score is 7 points per text (here 21, as the second text is repeated).
LM_imm_them.ReferencesAndLinks: Wechsler, D. (2004). WMS-III Escala de memoria de Wechsler-III. TEA ediciones.

LM_del_them.LongName: Logical Memory (delayed thematic), Wechsler Memory Scale-III-R (Spanish version).
LM_del_them.Instructions: Subjects listen to 2 short texts and are instructed to pay attention for later recall. The second text is repeated. For the score, a checklist of 7 general ideas is used 20 minutes after the reading. Subjects get 1 point for each remembered idea. The total score is 7 points per text (here 14).
LM_del_them.ReferencesAndLinks: Wechsler, D. (2004). WMS-III Escala de memoria de Wechsler-III. TEA ediciones.

PFT_average.Instructions: Subjects are asked to produce words starting with F, A, and S within one minute. The score is the average number of valid responses (i.e., minus repeats, proper nouns, or those outside the specified letters).
PFT_average.ReferencesAndLinks: Benton, A. L., Hamsher, K. D., & Sivan, A. B. (1994). Multilingual Aphasia Examination (3rd ed.). AJA Associates.

SFT_average.Instructions: Subjects are asked to produce words from the categories animals, fruits, and names within one minute. The score is the average number of valid responses (i.e., minus repeats or those outside the specified categories).
SFT_average.ReferencesAndLinks: Benton, A. L., Hamsher, K. D., & Sivan, A. B. (1994). Multilingual Aphasia Examination (3rd ed.). AJA Associates.

TMT_A_hits.Instructions: Subjects are asked to connect circled numbers in ascending order as quickly as possible. The score is the number of circled numbers (1-25) successfully connected within 180 seconds.
TMT_A_hits.ReferencesAndLinks: Reitan, R. M. (1958). Validity of the Trail Making Test as an indicator of organic brain damage. Perceptual and Motor Skills, 8(3), 271-276.

TMT_A_time.Instructions: Subjects are asked to connect circled numbers in ascending order as quickly as possible. The score is the number of seconds spent connecting the 25 circled numbers.
TMT_A_time.ReferencesAndLinks: Reitan, R. M. (1958). Validity of the Trail Making Test as an indicator of organic brain damage. Perceptual and Motor Skills, 8(3), 271-276.

TMT_B_hits.Instructions: Subjects are asked to alternately connect circled numbers and circled letters in ascending order as quickly as possible. The score is the number of circled numbers (1-13) and circled letters (A-L) successfully connected within 180 seconds.
TMT_B_hits.ReferencesAndLinks: Reitan, R. M. (1958). Validity of the Trail Making Test as an indicator of organic brain damage. Perceptual and Motor Skills, 8(3), 271-276.

TMT_B_time.Instructions: Subjects are asked to alternately connect circled numbers and circled letters in ascending order as quickly as possible. The score is the number of seconds spent connecting the circled numbers and letters.
TMT_B_time.ReferencesAndLinks: Reitan, R. M. (1958). Validity of the Trail Making Test as an indicator of organic brain damage. Perceptual and Motor Skills, 8(3), 271-276.

FAQ.ReferencesAndLinks: Pfeffer, R. I., Kurosaki, T. T., Harrah Jr, C. H., Chance, J. M., & Filos, S. (1982). Measurement of functional activities in older adults in the community. Journal of Gerontology, 37(3), 323-329.

GDS.ReferencesAndLinks: Yesavage, J. A., Brink, T. L., Rose, T. L., Lum, O., Huang, V., Adey, M., & Leirer, V. O. (1982). Development and validation of a geriatric depression screening scale: A preliminary report. Journal of Psychiatric Research, 17(1), 37-49.

MMSE.ReferencesAndLinks: Folstein, M. F., Folstein, S. E., & McHugh, P. R. (1975). 'Mini-mental state': A practical method for grading the cognitive state of patients for the clinician. Journal of Psychiatric Research, 12(3), 189-198.
